from db.session import SessionLocal
from db.models.mvp import CoreMatch
from sqlalchemy import func
db = SessionLocal\(\)
# Find pairs of teams with most meetings
from sqlalchemy import and_, or_
subq = db.query\(
    CoreMatch.home_team_id,
    CoreMatch.away_team_id,
    func.count\(\).label\('cnt'\)
\).filter\(CoreMatch.sport=='soccer', CoreMatch.status=='finished'\).group_by\(CoreMatch.home_team_id, CoreMatch.away_team_id\).having\(func.count\(\) > 3\).limit\(3\).all\(\)
for r in subq:

Bash(docker compose exec api python3 << 'EOF'
from db.session import SessionLocal
from db.models.mvp import CoreMatch
from sqlalchemy import func
db = SessionLocal\(\)
rows = \(db.query\(CoreMatch.home_team_id, CoreMatch.away_team_id, func.count\(\).label\('cnt'\)\)
    .filter\(CoreMatch.sport=='soccer', CoreMatch.status=='finished'\)
    .group_by\(CoreMatch.home_team_id, CoreMatch.away_team_id\)
    .order_by\(func.count\(\).desc\(\)\)
    .limit\(3\).all\(\)\)
for r in rows:
    print\(r.home_team_id, r.away_team_id, r.cnt\)
# Get a match ID for the top pair
if rows:
    m = db.query\(CoreMatch\).filter\(CoreMatch.home_team_id==rows[0].home_team_id, CoreMatch.away_team_id==rows[0].away_team_id, CoreMatch.status=='finished'\).first\(\)
    print\("Match ID:", m.id\)
EOF)
